Validate no data loss between pipeline steps

Source: R/sanity_checks.R
mysterycall_check_no_data_loss.Rd

Ensures row counts don't unexpectedly decrease between operations. Use at key checkpoints in data pipelines to catch silent filtering or joins that lose data.

Usage

mysterycall_check_no_data_loss(
  before,
  after,
  operation = "operation",
  expected_change = 0,
  tolerance = 0
)

Arguments

before

Row count before operation (or data frame)

after

Row count after operation (or data frame)

operation

Description of operation for error messages

expected_change

Expected change in rows. Defaults to 0 (no change). Use positive for operations that add rows (e.g., joins), negative for operations that should remove rows (e.g., deduplication).

tolerance

Maximum acceptable unexpected loss. Defaults to 0.

Value

Invisible TRUE if within expected change +/- tolerance

See also

Other utilities: mysterycall_download_file(), mysterycall_format_pct(), mysterycall_remove_constants(), mysterycall_remove_near_zero(), mysterycall_save_quality_table(), mysterycall_assess_data_quality(), mysterycall_check_api_response(), mysterycall_check_data_completeness(), mysterycall_check_dependencies(), mysterycall_check_no_limits(), mysterycall_estimate_resources(), mysterycall_export_with_backup(), mysterycall_preflight_check(), mysterycall_quality_tier(), mysterycall_resolve_path(), mysterycall_scan_for_limits(), mysterycall_standard_labels(), mysterycall_standard_palette(), mysterycall_use_quiet_logging()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Expect no data loss in cleaning
before <- nrow(raw_data)
clean_data <- mysterycall_clean_phase1(raw_data)
mysterycall_check_no_data_loss(before, clean_data, "Phase 1 cleaning")

# Expect deduplication to remove ~10 rows, allow +/-5
before <- nrow(data)
dedup_data <- deduplicate(data)
mysterycall_check_no_data_loss(before, dedup_data, "Deduplication",
                        expected_change = -10, tolerance = 5)
} # }
